raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Governor Gavin Newsom signed Assemblymember Matt Haney's AB 1136, establishing the Mixed Martial Arts Retirement Benefit Fund.",
    "The MMA Fighters Pension Fund will be financed through ticket sales, sports paraphernalia, and souvenirs, with $1 from each ticket sold going towards the fund.",
    "MMA fighters will become vested in the pension fund after participating in 12-14 fights, equating to about 39 scheduled rounds at Commission-regulated MMA events.",
    "Champion MMA fighters nationwide have shown support for the bill, highlighting the need for support for combat sports athletes.",
    "The bill aims to revolutionize the support and care for MMA fighters, ensuring their sacrifices are rewarded in their retirement years.",
    "California is the leading state for professional mixed martial arts, hosting the most fights and fighters in the country.",
    "The sport of MMA generates billions of dollars in revenue worldwide.",
    "Currently, MMA fighters lack a guaranteed source of income or retirement benefits after their careers end.",
    "Many MMA fighters suffer from long-term injuries due to their participation in the sport.",
    "The California State Athletic Commission will begin the process of creating the new fund in January 2024."
  ]
}
```
